libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No extra environment or system properties are required. Prefer coverage through public OGNL APIs such as parsing expressions, get/set property access, method calls, constructors, static access, maps/lists/arrays, and type conversion against test fixture POJOs. Treat SecurityManager-specific behavior as optional/fragile on newer JDKs and guard or avoid it unless directly needed.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #7430 [Automation] java run fails for ognl:ognl:3.3.5; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: OGNL has optional SecurityManager and expression-compiler/Javassist paths; they can be JDK-version-sensitive, especially on JDK 25.

If tests intentionally reflect into JDK internals or private JDK collection implementation details, additional --add-opens JVM args may become necessary, but that is not required for normal meaningful library coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: OGNL is a standalone expression/object-graph navigation library. The 3.3.5 artifact brings its required runtime dependency, javassist, transitively; normal meaningful coverage is reachable with POJO/map/list expression tests and no Docker image, extra Maven dependency, environment variable, or mandatory system property. The reported java-run failure is a changed/fragile test assertion in existing coverage, not missing setup.
